confirmations.reason: Custom Agents via TOML files let users define model configurations and instructions (system prompt, persona, behavior), which directly shapes the agent's system prompt and output style — a precise match for this capability.
The feature explicitly describes toggling individual MCP tools on/off and refreshing tool definitions, which is exactly the allow-listing / governance control over which MCP tools the agent may use.

Compaction addresses context-window management by summarizing/compacting accumulated history — this is context compaction, not prompt/context caching. Caching reuses stored context to cut latency and cost; compaction discards or summarizes old context to stay within limits. These are distinct mechanisms.

Agent Builder is described as a visual canvas for building multi-step agent workflows coordinating multiple agents, tools, and control flow — this directly realizes the capability of defining repeatable, named multi-step task sequences.

The Guardrails feature explicitly sanitizes inputs, validates/redacts outputs, and checks tool calls to block harmful content — this directly targets the kind of malicious instruction injection the capability describes.

Sandbox Agents provide an isolated Unix-like execution environment (filesystem, shell, packages), but this is a general sandboxed runtime — not specifically a dev-container integration (e.g., devcontainer.json / Docker-based defined dev environments). The capability is about a defined dev container standard; the feature is a proprietary sandbox surface.
Mobile Access via QR Code directly adds mobile (ChatGPT mobile app) as an access surface for the agent, which is precisely one of the surfaces enumerated by this capability.

SQLiteSession persists conversation history (messages/turns) across agent runs — this is durable session/history storage, not agent memory in the capability sense (i.e., semantic, summarized, or fact-based memory the agent actively recalls and updates). Persisting a conversation log is adjacent but not the same as persistent agent memory.
